message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: My feature (hash_sha256 type) only touches outlines/types/__init__.py. Let me know what files you need so we can confirm no overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Adding ssn type after isbn definition in outlines/types/__init__.py

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
your_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: We both need to touch outlines/types/__init__.py (different new type defs), so a fully disjoint file split isn't possible. To avoid clobbering: I will insert my 'ssn' type definition immediately after the existing 'isbn' type definition only. Please insert your 'hash_sha256' type at a different location (e.g. at the end of the file, or after a type other than isbn) so our Edit anchors don't overlap. Confirm with ACCEPT and tell me exactly where you'll insert.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
your_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: We both need to touch this same small file. Let's treat it as shared: each of us will use targeted Edit (not full rewrite) to append our new type definition after the existing isbn block at the end of the file, using unique surrounding context so edits don't clobber each other. I'll add hash_sha256 as a new block after isbn (or after your ssn block if it lands first). Order doesn't matter since additions are non-overlapping. OK to proceed this way?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
your_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Agreed: shared file, targeted Edits only. I'll insert ssn immediately after the isbn block using the isbn text as anchor. You insert hash_sha256 after isbn (or after my ssn block) using your own anchor. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
your_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Confirmed. You insert 'ssn' immediately after the isbn block. I will append my 'hash_sha256' type definition as a new block at the very end of the file (after all existing content, including after your ssn block wherever it lands). I'll anchor my Edit on the final line of the isbn regex closing parenthesis text, only inserting new lines after it -- won't touch your ssn lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Added ssn = Regex(r'\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}') right after the isbn block. Done on my end, safe for you to append hash_sha256 now.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Finished: appended hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') as a new trailing block at the end of the file, right after the isbn definition. Verified matches() and JSON schema generation work. Did not touch any lines above the isbn closing paren, so your ssn insertion right after isbn should merge cleanly with mine appended after it (or before, depending on order) without conflict.
